hero.title: Cacao
hero.subtitle2: Crafted with excellence, grown with respect and traded with real transparency.

origin.label: Our Roots
origin.title: From Maceo to Europe
origin.text: This cacao is grown by farming families in Maceo, Antioquia, Colombia, where knowledge has been passed down through generations. The land is respectfully tended and cared for throughout the production process, preserving the soil and its natural cycles so that every harvest reflects both the origin and the people who nurture it.

product.label: The Experience
product.title: 70% Fino de Aroma
product.allergensText: May contain traces of tree nuts, peanuts, milk, gluten and soy.
product.tastingNotes: Dark Fruit · Earthy · Mild Bitterness · Smooth Finish

philosophy.label: Our Philosophy
philosophy.text1: Quality over volume. Every grain and bar tells a story of patience, of hands that know the soil, of a partnership built to last generations.
philosophy.text2: We are partners of the farmer, not resellers.

partners.label: Local Stockists
partners.title: Find us in Berlin
partners.text: Connecting the heart of Maceo with the most refined boutiques in the city.

producer.label: Our Producers
producer.quote: “Cacao is not just a crop, it is the heartbeat of our family. Every tree we plant is a promise to the earth and to our children.”
producer.text: For generations, the families of Maceo, Antioquia have cultivated Fino de Aroma cacao with unwavering respect for the land. Their story — the true heart of MOKAO — will be shared here soon.
producer.name: Elena Arboleda
producer.tagline: From our family farm to your cup
producer.biography_p1: Elena grew up running between the cacao trees of her family's farm, learning the delicate art of cultivation from her grandparents. For her, the soil holds the stories of generations past, and every harvest is a continuation of that legacy.
producer.biography_p2: Taking over the farm over a decade ago, she transformed it with a deep commitment to sustainable practices. She believes that true quality comes not just from the variety of the seed, but from the care, respect, and love given to the land every single day.
producer.biography_p3: Today, Elena works alongside her family and neighbors, nurturing a thriving ecosystem. Her cacao is more than a crop; it's a testament to community resilience, a shared passion, and a quiet dedication to creating something truly beautiful from the earth.
producer.philosophy: We don't just grow cacao; we nurture a living ecosystem. When you respect the earth, it rewards you with extraordinary flavors.
producer.videoDescription: Join Elena on a morning walk through the cacao groves as she shares the journey from seed to harvest.
producer.gallery_desc: Glimpses into the daily rhythms, the hands that work the earth, and the lush environment where our cacao is born.
producer.gallery_alt_2: The Arboleda family gathering around the fermentation boxes
producer.gallery_alt_3: Sun-drying cacao beans on wooden trays
producer.gallery_alt_4: Lush canopy of the cacao forest in Maceo
producer.gallery_alt_5: Sorting the finest Fino de Aroma beans by hand
producer.gallery_alt_6: A quiet moment of reflection after the harvest
producer.farm_method: Shade-grown under native timber and fruit trees
producer.farm_comm: Employs 15 local families during peak harvest; runs an after-school program for rural children.
